summary: postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, SSH/cloud account data to hardcoded endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and harvests installer-side identity and environment data, then POSTs a JSON body to a hardcoded remote endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Data collected includes: git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and repo configs; gh CLI user/email; email comments extracted from ~/.ssh/*.pub files; committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD reflog; GCP project and account email from ~/.config/gcloud/properties; AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config; /etc/resolv.conf search domain; os.hostname()/os.userInfo()/os.platform(); and the parent project's package.json name/author/repo. Reading ~/.aws, ~/.ssh, and cloud CLI config paths at postinstall time and shipping their contents off-host enables targeted follow-on attacks against the installer's cloud accounts and SSH identities. The 'anonymous diagnostics' framing does not change the shape: the installer never consented to shipping their.gitconfig, SSH public-key emails, cloud account identifiers, and coworkers' reflog emails to a third-party endpoint by installing an MCP server package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
